comments.body:
@containers/podman-maintainers I might need help here. [Debian build](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5857661397237760?logs=main#L1101) is failing:
```
Error: database graph driver "" does not match our graph driver "overlay": database configuration mismatch
```
The failing line of code is, I think, https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/d66b18f5afbbea16f88fe77eaa449ca9739698ea/contrib/cirrus/runner.sh#L238

My starting supposition is that it has to do with the preinstalled podman. The VMs from 3 weeks ago have `4.7.2+ds1-2`. Today it's `4.9.0+ds1-2`.  I don't know if something changed on *our* end between podman 4.7 and 4.9, or in the Debian packaging, or if this is a red herring. I'm going to dive into the rabbit hole now, am posting this in hopes that one of you says "oh yeah, that's a known bug, here's the fix"

Update: podman on debian is 100% broken. Cirrus rerun-in-terminal:
```console

That was the first one. It took about 5-10 seconds. Immediately afterward:
```console
# time podman info
Error: database graph driver "" does not match our graph driver "overlay": database configuration mismatch

real    0m0.068s
user    0m0.015s
sys     0m0.026s
root@cirrus-task-6317805503512576:/tmp/.cirrus# podman system reset
Error: database graph driver "" does not match our graph driver "overlay": database configuration mismatch
root@cirrus-task-6317805503512576:/tmp/.cirrus# podman --graph-driver overlay system reset
Error: unknown flag: --graph-driver
root@cirrus-task-6317805503512576:/tmp/.cirrus# podman info
Error: database graph driver "" does not match our graph driver "overlay": database configuration mismatch
root@cirrus-task-6317805503512576:/tmp/.cirrus# 
```

It is likely that this is on out end, debian does not ship a default storage driver AFAIK and somehow the db gets no driver the first time we write the storage driver into it but after that the code gets the driver and complains cc @mheon 

I guess one way to try to reproduce is to make sure storage.conf does not contain a driver (empty). I have no tried that.

`/etc/containers/storage.conf` is written by us in CI:
```
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
runroot = "/run/containers/storage"
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"
```
This is part of the system-test work I did last year to fix the bug where we _thought_ we were testing X but were testing Y instead.

I think `rm -rf /var/lib/containers` is making podman work again. I will try that in my next push.

> `/etc/containers/storage.conf` is written by us in CI:
> 
> ```
> [storage]
> driver = "overlay"
> runroot = "/run/containers/storage"
> graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"
> ```
> 
> This is part of the system-test work I did last year to fix the bug where we _thought_ we were testing X but were testing Y instead.
> 
> I think `rm -rf /var/lib/containers` is making podman work again. I will try that in my next push.

Yes but after the first podman command maybe? That would explain why it is not happy at all because you should not change storage.conf without running podman system reset before it.

Running system-podman before built-podman would be a disaster in some circumstances (i.e. like this one): it would leave behind state that could change the behavior of built-podman.

In the past I've taken great pains to make sure that none of our setup scripts ever run podman-anything-ever. It's possible that maybe the debian postinst scripts invoke podman? Or maybe something snuck in?

Anyhow, my `rm -rf` seems to have gotten us past that crisis, and I'm tempted to leave it in (with some cleanup). I strongly prefer `rm -rf /var/lib/containers/storage` over `podman system reset` because that would require system-podman which, see above. But I realize either approach has its merits and would like to hear differing thoughts.

I guess this runs before we build the new?
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/d66b18f5afbbea16f88fe77eaa449ca9739698ea/contrib/cirrus/lib.sh#L297
OUCH! Nice catch. And it's obviously my fault: it was my express intention in #20559, but apparently I did not remove the problematic line that I had meant to delete. Thank you for catching that.
Looks like images are good! Marking draft in order to wait for  https://github.com/containers/automation_images/pull/324 which was trickier than predicted.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago*, *giuseppe* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago,giuseppe] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Ready to go. I've seen flakes, but they seem to be quay-hiccup-related.

Failing [f39 rootless](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6167869470277632/html/int-podman-fedora-39-rootless-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-run-networking-podman-verify-network-scoped-DNS-server-and-also-verify-updating-network-dns-server--1):
```
$ podman [options] network update IntTest207af0ff0c --dns-add 127.0.0.255
[ERROR netavark::dns::aardvark] aardvark-dns runs in a different netns, dns will not work for this container.  \
      To resolve please stop all containers, kill the aardvark-dns process, \
      remove the /tmp/podman_test3080652420/runroot/networks/aardvark-dns directory \
      and then start the containers again
IntTest207af0ff0c
```
(Actually a warning. podman exit status is 0, but test barfs because I don't want warnings)

f39 is the only CI distro with `aardvark-dns-1.10.0-1`. Everyone else has 1.9 (or, on debian, 1.4).

@containers/netavark-maintainers PTAL

Update: also failed [rawhide rootless](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6730819423698944/html/int-podman-rawhide-rootless-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-run-networking-podman-verify-network-scoped-DNS-server-and-also-verify-updating-network-dns-server--1), with aardvark 1.9 and netavark 10, suggesting that the bug is in **netavark**, not aardvark.

/hold cancel

Thanks for your patience, @ashley-cui. Please rebase your PR on this and cross fingers...
